text: When working on a software project, Git branching allows multiple developers to work simultaneously without interfering with each other's code. A common workflow involves creating *feature branches* from the main codebase. Each developer can build a new feature or fix a bug on their own branch. Once the work is complete, the next step is *merging*, where the changes are combined back into the main branch. To maintain quality, many teams use *pull requests* before merging. This lets other members review the code, discuss improvements, and catch errors. This structured *version control workflow* helps keep the project organized and ensures that new features are integrated smoothly and safely.
